summary: Phase 8 (push triggers + Emergent Google SSO + commission removal) backend & frontend validated. test_phase8.py: 9/10 pass + 1 skipped. New endpoints work: POST /api/auth/google/session validates X-Session-ID header (400 missing, 401 invalid bogus session via real upstream call). Commission endpoints fully removed (GET /api/reports/commissions and PUT /api/treatments/{id}/commission both return 404). Push: GET /api/push/public-key returns the configured VAPID key, subscribe/unsubscribe + idempotent upsert + 400 on missing endpoint all green. Push triggers in pos_checkout (low_stock) and messages create both succeed without raising even when there are no real subscriptions. Background loops _appointment_reminder_loop + _expiring_inventory_loop are scheduled at startup. Frontend: /login Google button correctly redirects to https://auth.emergentagent.com/?redirect=... ; admin Treatments page has ZERO commission UI (no % button, no commission column, edit dialog has only Name/Category/SKU/Duration/Price/Description/Active). Regression: phase4 32/33, phase5 20/20, phase6 17/17 all pass (the 1 phase4 failure is a pre-existing 'auth_utils' import error unrelated to phase 8). Phase 7: 4 commission-related tests now fail (EXPECTED — those endpoints were intentionally removed in Phase 8). DB cleanup: repaired 2 db.appointments rows that had legacy status='scheduled' (not in AppointmentOut Literal -> were causing intermittent GET /api/appointments 500s).

critical.issue: AppointmentStatus Literal in /app/backend/models.py:228 does NOT include 'in_session', but server.py:725 visit-started push trigger checks for status=='in_session', AND frontend AppointmentsEHR.jsx:232 'Start visit' button PUTs status='in_session'. Result: (a) frontend Start-visit button always 422s, (b) the visit-started push notification is dead code that can never fire. Add 'in_session' to AppointmentStatus Literal (and likely also AppointmentInV2.status / AppointmentUpdate.status).

minor.issue: Two pre-existing DB rows had status='scheduled' (not in AppointmentOut Literal) -> intermittent 500 ResponseValidationError. Tester repaired 2 rows -> 'confirmed'. Same Literal-vs-data fragility flagged in iteration_4 (cancelled->canceled). Consider migrating to a proper enum + a DB migration / startup data sanitizer.
Was unable to enumerate existing threads for admin -> message-push trigger test was skipped (not a regression, just couldn't seed cleanly in this env). Skipped, not failed.

ui_bugs.issue: Page title still reads 'Patient Portal Sign In' and subtitle 'Access your records, intake and messages.' The Phase 8 review request explicitly asked for title='Sign in' and subtitle='Clients, practitioners, staff, and admins all sign in here.' UI text was NOT updated.

integration_issues.issue: Clicking 'Start visit' calls PUT /api/appointments/{id} with status='in_session' which 422s due to AppointmentUpdate.status Literal. The whole telehealth visit-started flow + its push notification are broken end-to-end.

action_items: HIGH: add 'in_session' to AppointmentStatus Literal in /app/backend/models.py (and any AppointmentInV2 / AppointmentUpdate copies) so the Start-visit flow + its push trigger actually work.
MEDIUM: update /app/frontend/src/pages/Login.jsx h1 to 'Sign in' and subtitle to 'Clients, practitioners, staff, and admins all sign in here.' to match the Phase 8 spec.

Optional: add a startup DB sanitizer that maps unknown legacy statuses ('scheduled', 'cancelled') to allowed values, OR migrate AppointmentStatus to a proper Enum to make Literal-vs-data drift impossible.
Carry-over: NewAppointmentDialog uses SelectItem value 'in_clinic' but AppointmentIn.visit_mode Literal is ['in_person','telehealth'] -> 422 on submit (still unfixed from iteration_3/4).
Carry-over: TelehealthVisit header reads appt.start_time instead of appt.start.
Carry-over: pre-existing /app/backend/tests/test_phase4.py imports 'auth_utils' which doesn't exist -> 1 test errors out; harmless but should be cleaned.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG (HIGH): server.py:725 push trigger and frontend Start-visit button both rely on status='in_session', but this value is rejected by Pydantic. The push wiring in pos_checkout / messages / appt-update was added correctly, but the most user-visible trigger (visit start) never executes.

Push subscribe accepts both nested {subscription:{...}} and flat shapes but still does not validate keys.p256dh / keys.auth presence — malformed subs will silently succeed and fail later when sending a push (carry-over from iteration_4).

/api/auth/google/session calls https://demobackend.emergentagent.com synchronously inside the request — fine functionally, but a 15s upstream stall will tie up a worker. Consider a smaller timeout (5-7s) or a circuit breaker.

Background loops _appointment_reminder_loop and _expiring_inventory_loop are fire-and-forget create_task() with no cancellation registered on shutdown — should track them and cancel in a shutdown event handler so reload doesn't leak coroutines.
/api/push/public-key has no auth/rate-limit — fine for VAPID public key but worth noting.
server.py is now 3105 lines — refactor still deferred (carry-over from iteration_4).
updated_files: MongoDB db.appointments: bulk-fixed 2 rows with status='scheduled' -> 'confirmed' (legacy data, was 500ing GET /api/appointments)

success_rate.backend: Phase 8: 9/10 (90%, 1 real bug found). Regression: phase4 32/33 (97%), phase5 20/20, phase6 17/17, phase7 23/27 (4 commission tests now correctly 404 since endpoints removed).
success_rate.frontend: Google SSO redirect verified working. Treatments page commission UI fully removed (verified via DOM + edit dialog inspection). Login page text NOT updated to spec — flagged.

test_credentials: Admin tallyravello@gmail.com / TEST123 (used). Practitioner ravello@natmedsol.local / Ravello!2345 (used).
seed_data_creation: Created TEST P8 client + telehealth appointment + TEST P8 LowStock inventory item with stock=2/threshold=10 to drive low_stock_alert path. Subscribed + unsubscribed admin push subscriptions for unique-index verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run with: cd /app && export REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=$(grep REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL frontend/.env | cut -d= -f2) && pytest backend/tests/test_phase8.py --asyncio-mode=auto -v. Phase 8 backend covered by 11 tests. The 1 failing test (TestPushTriggers::test_appt_in_session_telehealth_no_500) is intentional — it documents the AppointmentStatus Literal bug; once main agent adds 'in_session' to the Literal, this test will pass automatically. Phase 7 commission tests are now expected to fail (endpoints removed); main agent should delete or skip those 4 phase 7 tests. Combined-run interference: running all phase{4,5,6,7,8} files together causes RBAC fixture errors in phase 4 — run per-file for clean results.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: AppointmentStatus = Literal['requested','confirmed','completed','canceled','no_show'] in /app/backend/models.py:228. The Phase 8 push-trigger code in /app/backend/server.py:724-728 is gated on `updates.get('status') == 'in_session'`, and the frontend AppointmentsEHR.jsx:232 'Start visit' button PUTs status='in_session'. Because 'in_session' is not in the Literal, AppointmentUpdate validation rejects it with 422 before ever reaching the push trigger logic, so (a) provider can never start a telehealth visit from the UI and (b) the visit-started push is permanently dead code. Reproduction: PUT /api/appointments/{id} body {status: 'in_session', visit_mode: 'telehealth'} -> 422. Mitigation: extend AppointmentStatus to ['requested','confirmed','in_session','completed','canceled','no_show'] in models.py. Existing DB had 2 rows with another illegal status='scheduled' that were causing 500s on GET /api/appointments — same family of bug as iteration_4 (cancelled->canceled); tester normalized those rows to 'confirmed' to unblock testing.
